column2: The German passenger airship Hindenburg was destroyed today while attempting to dock at the Lakehurst Naval Air Station in the United States. For reasons not yet determined, the airship was engulfed in flames and crashed to the ground, claiming 35 lives.\n\nDue to the scarcity and expense of helium, the Hindenburg, like all German passenger airships, was designed to use hydrogen, which provides greater lift but is also flammable. With public faith shaken by this disaster and the rise of much faster passenger aircraft, could this spell the end of the airship era?

Construction has finished on the Pentagon, a massive office complex that will house the entire United States War Department. Built across the Potomac from Washington, the Pentagon is the largest office building in the world with floor space of over 4,000,000 square feet. It was finished in less than a year and a half, and the first War Department employees moved into the building as early as eight months ago, while it was still under construction.\n\nThe Pentagon has the world's largest private switchboard, sufficient for a city of 125,000, and the cost to build it was roughly the same as a battleship. Yet, despite the building's vast size, the War Department can still not find enough space to fit all of its workers within the Pentagon's walls.

Leon Trotsky, once the top lieutenant and heir presumptive of Vladimir Ilyich Lenin, died today in a Mexico City hospital. He was attacked in his home with an ice axe by a Spanish national in the employ of the NKVD. He succumbed to his wounds one day later.\n\nAfter a failed power struggle with Joseph Stalin and his allies for control of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, Trotsky spent the thirties in exile, living in Turkey, France and Norway before finally settling in Mexico. He remained a constant critic of Stalin and the "degenerated workers' state" of the Soviet Union under his rule.

Wilhelm II, the last Kaiser of the German Empire, passed away today at the age of 82. Wilhelm fled into exile in the Netherlands at the conclusion of the First World War, after abdicating the throne and vowing never to return to Germany until the monarchy had been restored.\n\nHe assumed the throne in 1888, upon the deaths of his grandfather Wilhelm I and his father Frederick III, who only reigned for 99 days. His oldest son, the former Crown Prince Wilhelm, who commanded the 5th Army during the Great War, is now the new head of the House of Hohenzollern.

The famous aviator Amelia Earhart, the first woman to complete a solo flight across the Atlantic, has disappeared along with her navigator, Fred Noonan, near Howland Island in the middle of the Pacific Ocean. The two were attempting a flight around the entire world in their twin-engined Lockheed Electra plane when they vanished. A search for the missing aviators is now being conducted by the U.S. Navy and Coast Guard.
The Queen of the Air is lost.

The eleventh Olympic Games were recently held in Berlin, Germany. Attended by athletes from 49 different nations across the world, the 1936 Summer Olympics are the first in history to have enjoyed limited live television coverage.\n\nGerman athletes saw the most success, winning 33 gold medals, while the Americans came in second, with 24. Four of these were won by Jesse Owens, the single most successful athlete of the Games.

A recent radio adaptation of H.G. Wells' novel "War of the Worlds" in the United States has caused mass hysteria among its listeners. Directed and narrated by Orson Welles, the radio drama was a special Halloween episode of Mercury Theatre on the Air and described a seemingly unstoppable Martian invasion of the Earth.\n\nMany who caught only part of the broadcast believed it told of real events and reacted with panic. Experts attribute this to the tense international situation and the looming spectre of war that hangs over the nation.
The world is not coming to an end?

Mount Vesuvius, the Italian volcano famous for destroying the Roman city of Pompeii in AD 79, has erupted once again. The ash plumes can be seen from Naples, and although this eruption is not the volcano's most powerful, several villages near its base have suffered extensive damage.

As the first recon units advance into the city, the chimes of Big Ben echo throughout the deserted streets of London. The sound is soon drowned out by sporadic small-arms fire, and before long the bell itself is forever silenced by a tank round.\n\nParatroopers storm Buckingham Palace, but the Royal Family has already been evacuated well in advance of the attack. Still, the Union Flag is lowered and the flag of the conquerors hoisted in its place. London has fallen.

Like a terrible sandstorm sweeping in from the desert, enemy forces have descended on Cairo and claimed the city. The dust-covered armored columns roll past the Giza pyramids and the Great Sphinx on their way to new conquests.

Interrupted in the process of burning sensitive documents, the last CIA agents in Washington D.C. speed away in their black cars just as enemy armor advances north along the Potomac to take up firing positions. Even before the dust from the battle has settled, the first intelligence officers can be seen sifting through the rubble for intelligence.\n\nAs paratrooper detachments move to secure the White House and the Capitol, the Washington Monument is toppled during the course of the heavy fighting, reportedly by American combat engineers in a desperate attempt to block the enemy advance. It fails.
The dawn of a New Order.

Heavy street fighting erupts in the boroughs of New York City as enemy forces advance into Manhattan in a bid to capture the city. Tanks and armored cars smash through improvised anti-tank barriers and drive down Fifth Avenue, while troops move systematically from house to house, block by block, fighting a running battle against armed irregulars.\n\nFires rage out of control in the eastern parts of the city, lighting up the skyline as the fighting continues into the night. The Empire State Building is demolished by pioneers to silence the snipers firing down from its many windows. It seems as though the Statue of Liberty may emerge unscathed from the fighting, until it is raked by stray rockets, decapitating Lady Liberty.

As dawn breaks, tanks smash through hastily erected barriers on the streets of Moscow and half-tracks roll onto Red Square, unloading their cargo of troops. Thick, billowing smoke from the many fires raging in the suburbs rises into the air as the infantry storms the Kremlin. Grenades and flamethrowers are needed to fight the stubborn NKVD holdouts who seem intent on defending every inch of the complex.\n\nWith Moscow finally under control, explosions rock the city as timed explosives left by the retreating Soviets detonate. The Russian capital is left a burning ruin, but to the relief of the Soviet leadership, Lenin's embalmed corpse has been safely evacuated east.
Is this the end for Russia?

Leningrad, the birthplace of the Russian Revolution, has fallen. Sporadic fighting can still be heard from some suburbs, but a strange calm has settled over the rest of the ruined city.\n\nColumns of soldiers march past St. Isaac's Cathedral, whose great dome, painted grey earlier by the Soviets to avoid the attention of marauding bombers, is riddled with bullet holes and shrapnel impacts.
The struggle for the East continues.

The advancing enemy spearheads have seized Stalingrad, Stalin's namesake city on the banks of the Volga. Fighting their way through the ruined Stalingrad Tractor Factory and seizing the strategically vital Mamayev Kurgan heights, the last Soviet defenders across the Volga to its eastern shore.\n\nHoldouts from the Red Army fighting together with partisans retreat into sewers and basements and continue their struggle. The occupying soldiers struggling to consolidate their hold on the city invent a new name for this type of warfare.

Berlin, Adolf Hitler's envisioned "Welthauptstadt Germania", has finally succumbed to the advancing enemy after a pitched battle.\n\nGerman pioneers attempt the speedy demolition of the bridges, but only partially succeed, with enemy infantry still able to cross in large numbers. After a fierce battle inside the Reichstag building, the German flag is finally lowered on its roof.

Singapore, the "Gibraltar of the East," has been seized from the British forces. Although once considered an impregnable fortress owing to its massive coastal gun fortifications, this strategically vital port will now serve the enemy in any future campaigns in East Asia and beyond.

Australia's largest city has fallen. After offering stubborn resistance at the docks, the remaining Australian defenders retreat across the Sydney Harbour Bridge and assume new positions on Observatory Hill.\n\nAn attempt by engineers to demolish the bridge fails, and the hill is finally lost. A new flag now hangs from the Sydney Town Hall, the headquarters of the city's new military administration, and enemy military police maintain a watchful eye on the streets.

Enemy forces have stormed and taken Honolulu, the largest city on the Hawaiian island of O'ahu. The nearby U.S. naval base of Pearl Harbor has also fallen in an intense struggle.\n\nAfter retreating to Ford Island, beleaguered American sailors and Marines continue to offer stubborn resistance and surrender only after the island is subjected to an intense artillery and aerial bombardment. With this significant naval base now taken, the US navy will be unable to challenge in the Pacific.
A day which will live in infamy...

After landing on the American mainland, enemy forces have converged on the City of Angels and taken it. Significant fighting erupted all across Downtown Los Angeles, where pockets of resistance remained even as the invading troops fanned out and took control over other districts.\n\nAs the fighting dies down, save for the occasional sniper, Imperial troops march down Central Avenue in a parade to demonstrate the military might of Japan. Meanwhile a single Japanese fighter strafes the Hollywood sign in what must surely be considered an act of cultural desecration.

Our agents in various foreign research establisments have picked up several interesting details recently. While we have not managed to steal any actual designs, the different perspectives and techniques observed should speed up our own research efforts.

Our agents in various foreign factories have picked up several interesting details recently. While we have not managed to steal any actual designs, the different perspectives and techniques observed should speed up our own production.
Quantity has a quality all of its own!

Our recent effort to increase support for our party has really paid off recently, and a wave of popular support is sweeping the nation. Happy crowds throng the street whenever our party members make a speech.
Our recent effort to increase support for our ruling party has backfired horribly, and a wave of derision is sweeping the nation. Rotten fruit is thrown whenever our party members make a speech.
Depart, I say, and let us have done with you. In the name of God, go!
The looming threat of <$TARGET$> is driving our young men to sign up for the army in increasing numbers
They must be stopped, whatever the cost.
The looming threat of <$TARGET$> is too much for our people, they are increasingly loath to take any action that may bring down the wrath of <$TARGET$> upon them
Perhaps we should sit this one out?
By careful study of past invasions we can speed up the disembarkment of our troops.
Flat bottomed with a ramped bow, these craft are vitally needed to quickly and successfully land troops on hostile beaches.
Larger and better protected than landing craft, assault ships are even able to quickly unload tanks onto hostile beaches, which should make our amphibious operations much more likely to succeed.
By providing specialised craft to support invasions, such as fire support and anti-air, we can ensure our troops are better protected during landing operations.
ÏYThis unit is currently in amphibious transports. Its offensive capabilities are reduced and its defence improved by the transports' armour.Ï!

Fearing where the radical ruling party was taking the nation, reactionary elements of the army launch a coup to seize power in Spain. However, all does not go to plan and the Government retains control of more than half the country. A bloody war is now the only way to reunify the nation.

After the signing of the MolotovRibbentrop Pact, the occupation of the Baltic states and Eastern Poland, the USSR turned their attention onto their tiny neighbour, Finland. Expecting another round of easy expansion, the Soviets were instead shocked when their army initially proved woefully under-prepared for battles with the dug-in Finish troops. While this war may end in Russian victory, it will not be an easy fight.

Prepare for covert operations, sabotage and commando infiltration
Our secret service has recently captured an enemy agent and broken their entire spy ring. This will increase the efficiency of our counter-espionage nationwide for a time.

Counter-intelligence efforts suffered a blow recently as our agents' latest set of codes has fallen into the hands of the enemy. This will decrease the efficiency of our counter-espionage nationwide for a time.
Through a series of clever plots and at least one hair-raising adventure, one of our spies has stolen an enemy code book. Until they change their codes we will enjoy increased intel levels.
Sabotage the strategic resource in this province to reduce its effect.
Infiltrate to steal local battle plans and give us an intelligence advantage in the area.
Send in spies to do detailed scouting of the area before an advance.
Sabotage bridges, destroy road signs and generally make it a difficult place for the enemy to move through.
Works together with local underground partisans to commandeer a supply cache for the province.
We cannot lend-lease when we are a government in exile.
We cannot lend-lease to a government in exile.

Assault units are deployed specifically to take out key parts of a defence such as a fort or heavy bunker. They include combat engineers, assault groups and demoltion teams.

The Kampfgruppe was an ad-hoc combined arms formation, usually employing a combination of tanks, infantry, and artillery elements, generally organised for a particular task or operation. Kampfgruppen were generally referred to by either their commanding officer's name or the parent division. Hundreds of Kampfgruppen are documented to have taken part in operations and they left a significant impact in popular culture and the picture of the Second World War.
Recon units scout ahead, guard the flanks and perform the role of tactical eyes and ears for their formations
Fast moving units which are often combined with tank formations to create lethal mobile units
Special Forces are highly trained specialised units able to operate effectively in difficult terrain or climates
Armored units add punch, mobility, and protection to a division. With the Combined Arms technology, these units offer the greatest boost to combat efficiency.
Infantry is the basis for combined arms. Combine them with other arms for a combat bonus.
Direct Fire units are specialised units which engage targets within their line of sight.
Mixed Support units can add general utility, or certain special abilities, to a division to make it useful in different roles.
Armored units add punch, mobility, and protection to a division.
Artillery units can fire over other units or intervening objects to rain destruction on the enemy from a distance.
Transport units allow fast and efficient movement of entire units, their supplies, ammunition and fuel.
Motorized Transport units allow even faster and more efficient movement of our armies.
Anti Air artillery provides necessary on modern battlefield protection against enemy planes.
This category covers all units that wouldn't be considered front line units like garrisons, military police etc.
The standard Division HQ unit provides centralized command and control to other units in the division, thereby unlocking the potential for combined arms (CA) bonuses.
The Veteran Division HQ unit is similar to the standard Division HQ, but provides an additional combined arms (CA) bonus.
The Elite Division HQ unit is similar to the Veteran Division HQ, but provides an additional combined arms (CA) bonus.

Units with stronger armor than their enemy can penetrate will take reduced damage from their attacks, and do increased Organizational damage to them in return.
With our victory here our nation need no longer suffer under the corrupt rule of the Communists, Democrats, or Anarchists. We will grow strong under the able rule of General Franco!
Our defeat here condemns our nation to the misrule of Communists and Anarchists. The unified state of Spain and the Church are doomed.
Our victory over the Fascist forces of Franco ensures that the Spanish Republic will live on as a free and democratic state.
Our defeat here means a Fascist victory. Doubtlessly, Franco will join the rising powers of Italy and Germany, leading Spain into a doomed war against the remaining powers in Europe.
Leningrad is worth two victory points
This great victory will ensure the security of Leningrad for all time!
Our defeat here leaves Leningrad open to attack via Finland. No doubt even now the counter-revolutionaries plot against us!
Our victory over the Russian forces has once again defeated their imperialist advances towards our fair nation!

Our defeat has forced us to give up our border fortifications, it may prove that we must ally with anyone who has the strength and the willpower to fight the Soviet union if we ever hope to recover them.
There's no point in fighting anymore
Our defeat at the hands of Japan leaves us no choice but to cede our entire coastline to them. Perhaps one day we can recover it, but for now we must rebuild.

The coastal area of China contains the majority of their industry and resources. With it in our hands there is no real need to occupy the entire country or waste further time fighting the Chinese and their feuding warlord states.
